What Happened
in 1911?

Major events, music, film, tech & prices from 1911

The World in 1911
🌍EventsTriangle Shirtwaist Factory fire kills 146 workers (Mar 25) · Roald Amundsen's team first to reach South Pole (Dec 14) · Italy invades Libya in Italo-Turkish War (Sep 29) · Mona Lisa stolen from the Louvre in Paris (Aug 21) · Chinese Revolution overthrows Qing dynasty (Oct 10) · Standard Oil broken up by US Supreme Court (May 15)
🎵MusicAlexander's Ragtime Band – Billy Murray
🎬FilmLittle Nemo
💻TechCharles Kettering invented the electric automobile starter motor
🛒PricesBread $0.05 · Gas $0.24/gal · House $2,750

What major events happened in 1911?

1911 was a year of significant world events that shaped history. From political upheavals to cultural milestones, the year left a lasting mark on society. The music charts, cinema screens, and technological landscape of 1911 reflect the era's unique character. Prices for everyday goods like bread, gasoline, and homes give a vivid snapshot of the economic climate of the time.

Why is 1911 historically significant?

Each year carries its own defining moments and cultural touchstones. 1911 contributed unique chapters to the story of the 20th and 21st centuries. Whether you lived through 1911 or are researching it, the events, art, technology, and everyday costs of that year paint a rich picture of life at the time.

Frequently asked questions

What major events happened in 1911?

Key world events of 1911 include the milestones listed above. These ranged across politics, war, science, and culture, shaping the course of modern history.

What was the #1 song in 1911?

The top song of 1911 is listed in the music row above. Popular music in 1911 reflected the tastes and mood of the era.

What movies came out in 1911?

The standout film of 1911 is shown in the film row above. Cinema in 1911 captured the spirit of the times and influenced popular culture for years to come.

What did things cost in 1911?

In 1911, everyday prices included the figures shown in the prices row above for bread, gasoline, and the average house. These numbers illustrate how much purchasing power and the cost of living have changed over time.

What technology was new in 1911?

The tech highlight of 1911 is described in the technology row above. Innovations from 1911 often laid the groundwork for technologies we rely on today.
